root.description: Claude Code - starts an interactive session by default, use -p/--print for non-interactive output

flags.description: Additional directories to allow tool access to
Agent for the current session. Overrides the 'agent' setting.
JSON object defining custom agents (e.g. '{"reviewer": {"description": "Reviews code", "prompt": "You are a code reviewer"}}')
Enable bypassing all permission checks as an option, without it being enabled by default. Recommended only for sandboxes with no internet access.
Comma or space-separated list of tool names to allow (e.g. "Bash(git *) Edit")
Append a system prompt to the default system prompt

Minimal mode: skip hooks, LSP, plugin sync, attribution, auto-memory, background prefetches, keychain reads, and CLAUDE.md auto-discovery. Sets CLAUDE_CODE_SIMPLE=1. Anthropic auth is strictly ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or apiKeyHelper via --settings (OAuth and keychain are never read). 3P providers (Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry) use their own credentials. Skills still resolve via /skill-name. Explicitly provide context via: --system-prompt[-file], --append-system-prompt[-file], --add-dir (CLAUDE.md dirs), --mcp-config, --settings, --agents, --plugin-dir.
Beta headers to include in API requests (API key users only)
Enable SendUserMessage tool for agent-to-user communication
Enable Claude in Chrome integration
Continue the most recent conversation in the current directory
Bypass all permission checks. Recommended only for sandboxes with no internet access.
Enable debug mode with optional category filtering (e.g., "api,hooks" or "!1p,!file")
Write debug logs to a specific file path (implicitly enables debug mode)
Disable all skills
Comma or space-separated list of tool names to deny (e.g. "Bash(git *) Edit")
Effort level for the current session (low, medium, high, xhigh, max)

Move per-machine sections (cwd, env info, memory paths, git status) from the system prompt into the first user message. Improves cross-user prompt-cache reuse. Only applies with the default system prompt (ignored with --system-prompt).
Enable automatic fallback to specified model when the default model is overloaded or not available (only works with --print)
File resources to download at startup. Format: file_id:relative_path (e.g., --file file_abc:doc.txt file_def:img.png)
When resuming, create a new session ID instead of reusing the original (use with --resume or --continue)
Resume a session linked to a PR by PR number/URL, or open interactive picker with optional search term
Display help for command
Automatically connect to IDE on startup if exactly one valid IDE is available
Include all hook lifecycle events in the output stream (only works with --output-format=stream-json)
Include partial message chunks as they arrive (only works with --print and --output-format=stream-json)
Input format (only works with --print): "text" (default), or "stream-json" (realtime streaming input)
JSON Schema for structured output validation. Example: {"type":"object","properties":{"name":{"type":"string"}},"required":["name"]}
Maximum dollar amount to spend on API calls (only works with --print)
Load MCP servers from JSON files or strings (space-separated)
[DEPRECATED. Use --debug instead] Enable MCP debug mode (shows MCP server errors)
Model for the current session. Provide an alias for the latest model (e.g. 'sonnet' or 'opus') or a model's full name (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-8').
Set a display name for this session (shown in the prompt box, /resume picker, and terminal title)
Disable Claude in Chrome integration
Disable session persistence - sessions will not be saved to disk and cannot be resumed (only works with --print)
Output format (only works with --print): "text" (default), "json" (single result), or "stream-json" (realtime streaming)
Permission mode to use for the session
Load a plugin from a directory or .zip for this session only (repeatable: --plugin-dir A --plugin-dir B.zip)
Fetch a plugin .zip from a URL for this session only (repeatable: --plugin-url A --plugin-url B)

Print response and exit (useful for pipes). Note: The workspace trust dialog is skipped when Claude is run in non-interactive mode (via -p, or when stdout is not a TTY, e.g. piped or redirected output). Only use this in directories you trust. Settings files that fail validation are silently ignored in this mode (no error dialog is shown).
Enable prompt suggestions. In print/SDK mode, emits a prompt_suggestion message after each turn with a predicted next user prompt
Start an interactive session with Remote Control enabled (optionally named)
Prefix for auto-generated Remote Control session names
Re-emit user messages from stdin back on stdout for acknowledgment (only works with --input-format=stream-json and --output-format=stream-json)
Resume a conversation by session ID, or open interactive picker with optional search term
Use a specific session ID for the conversation (must be a valid UUID)
Comma-separated list of setting sources to load (user, project, local).
Path to a settings JSON file or a JSON string to load additional settings from
Only use MCP servers from --mcp-config, ignoring all other MCP configurations
System prompt to use for the session
Create a tmux session for the worktree (requires --worktree). Uses iTerm2 native panes when available; use --tmux=classic for traditional tmux.
Specify the list of available tools from the built-in set. Use "" to disable all tools, "default" to use all tools, or specify tool names (e.g. "Bash,Edit,Read").
Override verbose mode setting from config
Output the version number
Create a new git worktree for this session (optionally specify a name)

root.name: claude

entries.description: Add a working directory for file access during the current session.
entries.name: /add-dir <path>

entries.description: Manage agent (subagent) configurations.
entries.name: /agents

entries.description: Spawn a Claude Code on the web session that watches the current branch's PR and pushes fixes when CI fails or reviewers comment. Requires the gh CLI.
entries.name: /autofix-pr [prompt]

entries.description: Detach the current session to run as a background agent and free the terminal. Alias: /bg.
entries.name: /background [prompt]

entries.description: (bundled skill) Orchestrate large-scale changes in parallel: decompose into 5–30 units, one background subagent per git worktree. Requires a git repository.
entries.name: /batch <instruction>

entries.description: Create a branch of the current conversation at this point, preserving the original. Alias: /fork.
entries.name: /branch [name]

entries.description: Ask a quick side question without adding to the conversation.
entries.name: /btw <question>

entries.description: Configure Claude in Chrome settings.
entries.name: /chrome

entries.description: (bundled skill) Load Claude API reference material for your project's language; migrate API code to a newer model, or onboard a Managed Agent.
entries.name: /claude-api [migrate|managed-agents-onboard]

entries.description: Start a new conversation with empty context; the previous one stays in /resume. Aliases: /reset, /new.
entries.name: /clear [name]

entries.description: (bundled skill) Review the current diff for bugs and cleanups; --fix applies findings, --comment posts PR comments, ultra runs a cloud review.
entries.name: /code-review [low|medium|high|xhigh|max|ultra] [--fix] [--comment] [target]

entries.description: Set the prompt-bar color for the current session.
entries.name: /color [color|default]

entries.description: Free up context by summarizing the conversation so far; optional focus instructions.
entries.name: /compact [instructions]

entries.description: Open the Settings interface (theme, model, output style, and other preferences). Alias: /settings.
entries.name: /config

entries.description: Visualize current context usage as a colored grid with optimization suggestions.
entries.name: /context [all]

entries.description: Copy the last assistant response (or the Nth-latest) to the clipboard.
entries.name: /copy [N]

entries.description: Alias for /usage.
entries.name: /cost

entries.description: (bundled skill) Enable debug logging and troubleshoot by reading the session debug log.
entries.name: /debug [description]

entries.description: (bundled workflow) Fan out web searches, fetch and cross-check sources, and synthesize a cited report.
entries.name: /deep-research <question>

entries.description: Continue the current session in the Claude Code Desktop app (macOS/Windows, subscription). Alias: /app.
entries.name: /desktop

entries.description: Open an interactive diff viewer showing uncommitted changes and per-turn diffs.
entries.name: /diff

entries.description: Diagnose and verify your Claude Code installation and settings.
entries.name: /doctor

entries.description: Set the model effort level (low/medium/high/xhigh/max/ultracode); auto resets to the model default.
entries.name: /effort [level|auto]

entries.description: Exit the CLI (detaches an attached background session). Alias: /quit.
entries.name: /exit

entries.description: Export the current conversation as plain text.
entries.name: /export [filename]

entries.description: Toggle fast mode on or off.
entries.name: /fast [on|off]

entries.description: Submit feedback, report a bug, or share your conversation. Aliases: /bug, /share.
entries.name: /feedback [report]

entries.description: (bundled skill) Scan transcripts for common read-only calls and add an allowlist to project settings.
entries.name: /fewer-permission-prompts

entries.description: Toggle the focus view (last prompt, tool-call summary, final response). Fullscreen only.
entries.name: /focus

entries.description: Set a goal Claude keeps working toward across turns until met; clear removes it.
entries.name: /goal [condition|clear]

entries.description: Write a JS heap snapshot and memory breakdown for diagnosing high memory usage.
entries.name: /heapdump

entries.description: Show help and available commands.
entries.name: /help

entries.description: View hook configurations for tool events.
entries.name: /hooks

entries.description: Manage IDE integrations and show status.
entries.name: /ide

entries.description: Initialize the project with a CLAUDE.md guide.
entries.name: /init

entries.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions.
entries.name: /insights

entries.description: Set up the Claude GitHub Actions app for a repository.
entries.name: /install-github-app

entries.description: Install the Claude Slack app via the OAuth flow.
entries.name: /install-slack-app

entries.description: Open or create your keybindings configuration file.
entries.name: /keybindings

entries.description: Sign in to your Anthropic account.
entries.name: /login

entries.description: Sign out from your Anthropic account.
entries.name: /logout

entries.description: (bundled skill) Run a prompt repeatedly while the session stays open; omit the interval to self-pace. Alias: /proactive.
entries.name: /loop [interval] [prompt]

entries.description: Manage MCP server connections and OAuth authentication.
entries.name: /mcp

entries.description: Edit CLAUDE.md memory files; enable/disable and view auto-memory entries.
entries.name: /memory

entries.description: Show a QR code to download the Claude mobile app. Aliases: /ios, /android.
entries.name: /mobile

entries.description: Switch the AI model and save it as the default for new sessions.
entries.name: /model [model]

entries.description: Share a free week of Claude Code with friends (only if eligible).
entries.name: /passes

entries.description: Manage allow/ask/deny rules for tool permissions. Alias: /allowed-tools.
entries.name: /permissions

entries.description: Enter plan mode directly from the prompt.
entries.name: /plan [description]

entries.description: Manage Claude Code plugins.
entries.name: /plugin

entries.description: Discover Claude Code features through quick interactive lessons.
entries.name: /powerup

entries.description: View and update your privacy settings (Pro/Max).
entries.name: /privacy-settings

entries.description: Open Claude FM lo-fi radio in your browser.
entries.name: /radio

entries.description: Generate a one-line summary of the current session on demand.
entries.name: /recap

entries.description: View the changelog in an interactive version picker.
entries.name: /release-notes

entries.description: Reload active plugins to apply pending changes without restarting.
entries.name: /reload-plugins

entries.description: Re-scan skill and command directories so on-disk changes apply without restarting.
entries.name: /reload-skills

entries.description: Make this session available for remote control from claude.ai. Alias: /rc.
entries.name: /remote-control

entries.description: Configure the default remote environment for web sessions.
entries.name: /remote-env

entries.description: Rename the current session and show the name on the prompt bar.
entries.name: /rename [name]

entries.description: Resume a conversation by ID or name, or open the session picker. Alias: /continue.
entries.name: /resume [session]

entries.description: Review a pull request locally in your current session.
entries.name: /review [PR]

entries.description: Rewind the conversation and/or code to a previous point, or summarize from a message. Aliases: /checkpoint, /undo.
entries.name: /rewind

entries.description: (bundled skill) Launch and drive your project's app to see a change working in the running app.
entries.name: /run

entries.description: (bundled skill) Write a per-project skill teaching /run and /verify how to launch and drive your app.
entries.name: /run-skill-generator

entries.description: Toggle sandbox mode (supported platforms only).
entries.name: /sandbox

entries.description: Create, update, list, or run routines on Anthropic-managed cloud infrastructure. Alias: /routines.
entries.name: /schedule [description]

entries.description: Adjust mouse-wheel scroll speed interactively (fullscreen only).
entries.name: /scroll-speed

entries.description: Analyze pending changes on the current branch for security vulnerabilities.
entries.name: /security-review

entries.description: Configure Amazon Bedrock auth, region, and model pins (visible when CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK=1).
entries.name: /setup-bedrock

entries.description: Configure Google Vertex AI auth, project, region, and model pins (visible when CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX=1).
entries.name: /setup-vertex

entries.description: (bundled skill) Review changed code for cleanup opportunities and apply fixes; does not hunt for bugs (use /code-review for that).
entries.name: /simplify [target]

entries.description: List available skills.
entries.name: /skills

entries.description: Alias for /usage (opens on the Stats tab).
entries.name: /stats

entries.description: Open the Settings interface (Status tab): version, model, account, connectivity.
entries.name: /status

entries.description: Configure Claude Code's status line.
entries.name: /statusline

entries.description: Order Claude Code stickers.
entries.name: /stickers

entries.description: Stop the current background session (only while attached; transcript and worktree are kept).
entries.name: /stop

entries.description: List and manage background tasks. Also available as /bashes.
entries.name: /tasks

entries.description: Generate a team onboarding guide from your Claude Code usage history.
entries.name: /team-onboarding

entries.description: Pull a Claude Code on the web session into this terminal. Also /tp.
entries.name: /teleport

entries.description: Configure terminal keybindings for Shift+Enter and other shortcuts.
entries.name: /terminal-setup

entries.description: Change the color theme (auto/light/dark, daltonized, ANSI, and custom themes).
entries.name: /theme

entries.description: Set the terminal UI renderer and relaunch into it with your conversation intact.
entries.name: /tui [default|fullscreen]

entries.description: Draft a plan in an ultraplan session, review it in your browser, then execute remotely or send it back to your terminal.
entries.name: /ultraplan <prompt>

entries.description: Run a deep multi-agent cloud code review; the preferred invocation is now /code-review ultra.
entries.name: /ultrareview [PR]

entries.description: Open the upgrade page to switch to a higher plan tier.
entries.name: /upgrade

entries.description: Show session cost, plan usage limits, and activity stats. Aliases: /cost, /stats.
entries.name: /usage

entries.description: Configure usage credits to keep working when you hit a limit (was /extra-usage).
entries.name: /usage-credits

entries.description: (bundled skill) Confirm a code change does what it should by building, running, and observing your app.
entries.name: /verify

entries.description: Toggle voice dictation, or enable it in a specific mode (requires a Claude.ai account).
entries.name: /voice [hold|tap|off]

entries.description: Connect your GitHub account to Claude Code on the web using your local gh credentials.
entries.name: /web-setup

entries.description: Open the workflow progress view to watch, pause, resume, or save workflows.
entries.name: /workflows

supplement_sections.note: Type `/` in a session to see the commands available to you; availability varies by platform, plan, and environment. MCP servers can expose prompts as `/mcp__<server>__<prompt>` commands, discovered dynamically from connected servers. Entries marked "(bundled skill)" / "(bundled workflow)" are prompts Claude can also invoke automatically. Excludes `/vim` (removed v2.1.92) and `/pr-comments` (removed v2.1.91), which are absent from the installed CLI.
supplement_sections.title: In-REPL slash commands

entries.description: Claude Code's standard system prompt, designed to complete software-engineering tasks efficiently.
entries.name: Default

entries.description: Executes immediately, makes reasonable assumptions instead of pausing for routine decisions, and prefers action over planning (you still see permission prompts).
entries.name: Proactive

entries.description: Adds educational "Insights" between steps to explain implementation choices and codebase patterns.
entries.name: Explanatory

entries.description: Collaborative learn-by-doing: shares "Insights" and adds TODO(human) markers for you to implement small pieces of code yourself.
entries.name: Learning

supplement_sections.note: Output styles modify Claude Code's system prompt (role, tone, output format). Select one via `/config` → Output style; the choice persists as the `outputStyle` setting in .claude/settings.local.json, and takes effect after /clear or a new session. The standalone `/output-style` command was deprecated in v2.1.73 and removed in v2.1.91. Custom styles can be authored as Markdown files under ~/.claude/output-styles, .claude/output-styles, or a managed-settings directory.
supplement_sections.title: Output styles
